pci_dss.overall_message: 20 PCI DSS requirement(s) are failing. Immediate remediation required before payment processing can be certified.

requirements.title: Software development practices prevent introduction of security vulnerabilities
requirements.description: BOLA vulnerabilities arise from missing object-level access control checks. PCI DSS 6.2.4 mandates secure coding practices that prevent authorization bypass in all custom code.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API1:2023 (Broken Object Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Unauthorized access to object detected via BOLA test
findings.recommendation: Validate resource ownership on every GET/PATCH/DELETE by object ID.

requirements.remediation: Implement server-side authorization checks on every API endpoint. Validate the authenticated user has permission to access the specific resource object. Use ABAC or RBAC policies consistently.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed
requirements.description: BOLA must be detected through security testing (DAST/SAST) and remediated before deployment to production.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API1:2023 (Broken Object Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Unauthorized access to object detected via BOLA test
findings.recommendation: Validate resource ownership on every GET/PATCH/DELETE by object ID.

requirements.remediation: Add automated BOLA testing to CI/CD pipeline. Scan every pull request for object-level authorization gaps.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Access is granted on a need-to-know basis
requirements.description: BOLA directly violates the need-to-know principle by allowing unauthorized access to objects outside the user's scope.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API1:2023 (Broken Object Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Unauthorized access to object detected via BOLA test
findings.recommendation: Validate resource ownership on every GET/PATCH/DELETE by object ID.

requirements.remediation: Enforce least-privilege access. Map every API endpoint to required roles. Verify ownership before returning resources.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: All user IDs and authentication credentials are managed
requirements.description: Broken authentication allows attackers to compromise authentication tokens or exploit implementation flaws to hijack sessions.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 3 finding(s) related to API2:2023 (Broken Authentication).

findings.issue: API key transmitted in URL query parameter
findings.recommendation: Move API keys to Authorization header. Never include secrets in URLs.
Reject authentication over plaintext HTTP. Enforce HTTPS for all auth.

findings.issue: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
findings.recommendation: Implement rate limiting: max 5 auth attempts per 15 minutes per IP.

requirements.remediation: Implement strong authentication mechanisms. Use short-lived JWTs with rotation. Enforce MFA for privileged accounts. Implement account lockout after failed attempts.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(d) — Processes for regularly testing security

requirements.title: All user access to system components is authenticated
requirements.description: Every API endpoint must require proper authentication. No endpoint should be accessible without valid credentials.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 3 finding(s) related to API2:2023 (Broken Authentication).

findings.issue: API key transmitted in URL query parameter
findings.recommendation: Move API keys to Authorization header. Never include secrets in URLs.
Reject authentication over plaintext HTTP. Enforce HTTPS for all auth.

findings.issue: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
findings.recommendation: Implement rate limiting: max 5 auth attempts per 15 minutes per IP.

requirements.remediation: Audit all API endpoints for authentication requirements. Remove public access to internal APIs. Implement API gateway authentication.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(d) — Processes for regularly testing security

requirements.title: Minimum password complexity and strength
requirements.description: Weak password policies enable brute-force attacks against API authentication endpoints.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 3 finding(s) related to API2:2023 (Broken Authentication).

findings.issue: API key transmitted in URL query parameter
findings.recommendation: Move API keys to Authorization header. Never include secrets in URLs.
Reject authentication over plaintext HTTP. Enforce HTTPS for all auth.

findings.issue: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
findings.recommendation: Implement rate limiting: max 5 auth attempts per 15 minutes per IP.

requirements.remediation: Enforce minimum 12-character passwords with complexity requirements. Implement credential stuffing protection.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(d) — Processes for regularly testing security

requirements.title: MFA for all non-console administrative access
requirements.description: Administrative API access without MFA is a critical vulnerability.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 3 finding(s) related to API2:2023 (Broken Authentication).

findings.issue: API key transmitted in URL query parameter
findings.recommendation: Move API keys to Authorization header. Never include secrets in URLs.
Reject authentication over plaintext HTTP. Enforce HTTPS for all auth.

findings.issue: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
findings.recommendation: Implement rate limiting: max 5 auth attempts per 15 minutes per IP.

requirements.remediation: Implement MFA for all administrative and privileged API access. Use hardware tokens or TOTP.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(d) — Processes for regularly testing security

requirements.title: Software development practices prevent introduction of security vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Mass assignment and excessive data exposure violate secure coding practices by exposing more data than the client needs.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API3:2023 (Broken Object Property Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Excessive data exposure: full user object returned including password hash
findings.recommendation: Use DTOs. Strip sensitive fields (password, internal_id) from responses.

requirements.remediation: Use DTOs (Data Transfer Objects) with allowlists for every API endpoint. Never bind request bodies directly to internal models. Strip sensitive fields from responses.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Access is granted on a need-to-know basis
requirements.description: Exposing object properties beyond the user's authorization level violates need-to-know access control.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API3:2023 (Broken Object Property Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Excessive data exposure: full user object returned including password hash
findings.recommendation: Use DTOs. Strip sensitive fields (password, internal_id) from responses.

requirements.remediation: Implement field-level authorization. Define per-role field access policies. Validate all input properties against schema.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: PAN is secured wherever it is stored
requirements.description: If cardholder data properties are exposed through API responses, PAN data may leak to unauthorized consumers.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API3:2023 (Broken Object Property Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Excessive data exposure: full user object returned including password hash
findings.recommendation: Use DTOs. Strip sensitive fields (password, internal_id) from responses.

requirements.remediation: Mask PAN in all API responses. Use tokenization for card data. Audit all endpoints that return payment-related objects.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks
requirements.description: Unrestricted resource consumption leads to denial of service, directly impacting payment processing availability and system integrity.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Implement rate limiting per user, per IP, and per endpoint. Set maximum request/response sizes. Implement request queuing for expensive operations.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(c) — Ability to restore availability after incident

requirements.title: Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies
requirements.description: Resource exhaustion attacks generate distinctive traffic patterns that should trigger alerts.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Implement anomaly detection for request volume. Alert on unusual resource consumption patterns. Log rate limit violations.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(c) — Ability to restore availability after incident

requirements.title: Vulnerabilities are managed by risk ranking
requirements.description: Denial of service risk must be assessed and prioritized in vulnerability management.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Include DoS resilience in vulnerability assessments. Test API endpoints for resource consumption limits.
gdpr_articles: Article 32(1)(c) — Ability to restore availability after incident

requirements.title: Access is granted on a need-to-know basis
requirements.description: BFLA allows regular users to invoke administrative functions, directly violating need-to-know access control.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API5:2023 (Broken Function Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Admin endpoint accessible without admin role verification
findings.recommendation: Enforce role-based authorization on all /admin/* routes.

requirements.remediation: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) at the function level. Deny by default. Verify caller role before executing any administrative operation.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Access to system components is assigned based on job function
requirements.description: Administrative API functions must be restricted to users with appropriate job functions.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API5:2023 (Broken Function Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Admin endpoint accessible without admin role verification
findings.recommendation: Enforce role-based authorization on all /admin/* routes.

requirements.remediation: Define explicit role-to-function mappings. Remove admin endpoints from public API documentation. Implement API gateway policies.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: All access is authorized and authenticated
requirements.description: Every function-level operation must verify both authentication and authorization.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API5:2023 (Broken Function Level Authorization).

findings.issue: Admin endpoint accessible without admin role verification
findings.recommendation: Enforce role-based authorization on all /admin/* routes.

requirements.remediation: Add authorization middleware to all admin routes. Implement function-level permission checks. Log all unauthorized access attempts.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Software development practices prevent introduction of security vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Business logic abuse is a design-level vulnerability that must be prevented through secure design practices.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Implement CAPTCHA or proof-of-work for sensitive flows. Add device fingerprinting. Detect automation patterns. Limit transaction velocity.

requirements.title: Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks
requirements.description: Business logic abuse can result in financial loss through automated exploitation of payment and transaction flows.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Implement WAF rules for automation detection. Add business-level rate limiting. Monitor for unusual transaction patterns.

requirements.title: Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies
requirements.description: Business logic abuse patterns are detectable through log analysis and anomaly detection.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Log all sensitive business operations. Implement real-time anomaly detection. Alert on suspicious transaction velocity.

requirements.title: Software development practices prevent introduction of security vulnerabilities
requirements.description: SSRF allows attackers to pivot to internal systems, potentially accessing cardholder data environments (CDE).
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API7:2023 (Server Side Request Forgery).

findings.issue: SSRF: user-controlled URL parameter fetched without validation
findings.recommendation: Validate and allowlist URLs. Block internal IP ranges.

requirements.remediation: Validate and sanitize all user-supplied URLs. Use allowlists for permitted domains. Block requests to internal IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x).
gdpr_articles: Article 33 — Notification of breach to supervisory authority

requirements.title: Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted
requirements.description: SSRF can bypass network segmentation by making the server initiate connections to internal resources.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API7:2023 (Server Side Request Forgery).

findings.issue: SSRF: user-controlled URL parameter fetched without validation
findings.recommendation: Validate and allowlist URLs. Block internal IP ranges.

requirements.remediation: Implement network-level controls preventing servers from reaching internal resources. Use egress filtering. Isolate the CDE from application servers.
gdpr_articles: Article 33 — Notification of breach to supervisory authority

requirements.title: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted
requirements.description: SSRF in the CDE could exfiltrate cardholder data to attacker-controlled servers.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 1 finding(s) related to API7:2023 (Server Side Request Forgery).

findings.issue: SSRF: user-controlled URL parameter fetched without validation
findings.recommendation: Validate and allowlist URLs. Block internal IP ranges.

requirements.remediation: Restrict outbound connections from CDE components. Monitor for unusual outbound traffic. Use DNS filtering.
gdpr_articles: Article 33 — Notification of breach to supervisory authority

requirements.title: Configuration standards are developed and implemented
requirements.description: Missing security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options) indicate non-compliance with configuration standards.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 5 finding(s) related to API8:2023 (Security Misconfiguration).

findings.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS.
Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
Set X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins. Remove wildcard.
Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy to prevent XSS.

requirements.remediation: Implement security header standards: HSTS, Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy. Remove debug endpoints.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Only necessary services are enabled
requirements.description: Unnecessary services and features increase the attack surface.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 5 finding(s) related to API8:2023 (Security Misconfiguration).

findings.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS.
Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
Set X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins. Remove wildcard.
Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy to prevent XSS.

requirements.remediation: Disable debug mode, unused HTTP methods, and directory listings. Remove default pages and documentation from production.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: Software development practices prevent introduction of security vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Security misconfigurations must be caught during development through automated configuration validation.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 5 finding(s) related to API8:2023 (Security Misconfiguration).

findings.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS.
Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
Set X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins. Remove wildcard.
Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy to prevent XSS.

requirements.remediation: Add security header validation to CI/CD pipeline. Use infrastructure-as-code with security baselines. Automate configuration scanning.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: All non-console administrative access is encrypted
requirements.description: API administrative interfaces must be encrypted and properly configured.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 5 finding(s) related to API8:2023 (Security Misconfiguration).

findings.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS.
Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
Set X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking.
Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins. Remove wildcard.
Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy to prevent XSS.

requirements.remediation: Enforce TLS for all admin APIs. Disable plaintext HTTP administrative access. Implement certificate pinning where appropriate.
gdpr_articles: Article 25 — Data Protection by Design and by Default

requirements.title: An inventory of bespoke and custom software is maintained
requirements.description: Server and technology disclosure headers reveal software inventory to attackers. Shadow APIs bypass security controls.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 2 finding(s) related to API9:2023 (Improper Inventory Management).

findings.recommendation: Remove or obscure Server header in nginx configuration.
Remove X-Powered-By header via app.disable('x-powered-by').

requirements.remediation: Remove Server and X-Powered-By headers. Maintain a comprehensive API inventory. Decommission deprecated endpoints. Implement API versioning.

requirements.title: Each PCI DSS requirement is managed according to a targeted risk analysis
requirements.description: Technology disclosure and shadow APIs must be assessed as part of risk management.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 2 finding(s) related to API9:2023 (Improper Inventory Management).

findings.recommendation: Remove or obscure Server header in nginx configuration.
Remove X-Powered-By header via app.disable('x-powered-by').

requirements.remediation: Document all API endpoints. Conduct regular API discovery scans. Implement API lifecycle management.

requirements.title: Vulnerabilities are managed by risk ranking
requirements.description: Undocumented APIs are not included in vulnerability management, creating blind spots.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 2 finding(s) related to API9:2023 (Improper Inventory Management).

findings.recommendation: Remove or obscure Server header in nginx configuration.
Remove X-Powered-By header via app.disable('x-powered-by').

requirements.remediation: Include all discovered APIs in vulnerability scanning. Prioritize shadow API discovery. Use DevPulse Shadow API Discovery for continuous monitoring.

requirements.title: System configuration standards are updated
requirements.description: API inventory must be kept current with configuration standards.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan detected 2 finding(s) related to API9:2023 (Improper Inventory Management).

findings.recommendation: Remove or obscure Server header in nginx configuration.
Remove X-Powered-By header via app.disable('x-powered-by').

requirements.remediation: Automate API inventory updates. Track API changes through version control. Maintain living API documentation.

requirements.title: Software development practices prevent introduction of security vulnerabilities
requirements.description: Trusting third-party API responses without validation introduces injection vulnerabilities and data integrity issues.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Validate and sanitize all third-party API responses. Implement schema validation. Use prepared statements for data persistence. Treat external API data as untrusted input.

requirements.title: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed
requirements.description: Third-party API integrations must be included in security testing scope.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Include third-party API integrations in SAST/DAST testing. Monitor for changes in third-party API schemas. Implement contract testing.

requirements.title: Each PCI DSS requirement is managed according to a targeted risk analysis
requirements.description: Third-party API dependencies must be assessed for security risk.
requirements.evidence: DevPulse DAST scan found no violations of this requirement.
requirements.remediation: Conduct security assessments of all third-party API integrations. Implement API dependency risk scoring. Monitor for third-party API security advisories.

checks.title: Purpose Limitation
checks.evidence: 1 finding(s) detected related to 'Purpose Limitation': Excessive data exposure: full user object returned including password hash
checks.remediation: Audit all API endpoints to ensure personal data is only used for its stated purpose. Implement data usage logging.

checks.title: Data Minimisation
checks.evidence: 1 finding(s) detected related to 'Data Minimisation': Excessive data exposure: full user object returned including password hash
checks.remediation: Implement response filtering. Return only required fields. Audit API response schemas.

checks.title: Integrity and Confidentiality
checks.evidence: 1 finding(s) detected related to 'Integrity and Confidentiality': Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
checks.remediation: Implement encryption, access controls, and integrity checks on all personal data processing endpoints.

checks.title: Right to Erasure
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Right to Erasure.

checks.title: Data Protection by Design
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Data Protection by Design.

checks.title: Records of Processing Activities
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Records of Processing Activities.

checks.title: Encryption and Pseudonymisation
checks.evidence: 1 finding(s) detected related to 'Encryption and Pseudonymisation': HTTP instead of HTTPS detected
checks.remediation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ on all API endpoints. Implement HSTS. Use pseudonymisation for personal data in non-production environments.

checks.title: Ongoing Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Ongoing Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability.

checks.title: Restore Availability
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Restore Availability.

checks.title: Breach Notification
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Breach Notification.

checks.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
checks.evidence: No violations detected for Data Protection Impact Assessment.

attestation.note: This report is generated from automated security scanning. For formal PCI DSS certification, engage a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA). This report provides evidence for your QSA engagement.
